Items.Key: format string vulnerability in format_string template function|format string vulnerability|cwe-134|medium|0,8|enhanced_calc.cpp|218|223|string_utils::format_string|template<typename... args> inline std::string format_string(const char* fmt, args... args) { char buffer[buf_size]; snprintf(buffer, buf_size, fmt, args...); return buffer; }|the fmt parameter is passed directly to snprintf as the format string. if called with user-controlled format strings, attackers can leak stack data or cause crashes.|call to format_string with user-controlled first argument|validate that fmt contains only expected format specifiers, or use a whitelist approach for format strings.->format string vulnerability in format_string template function|format string vulnerability|cwe-134|medium|0,8|enhanced_calc.cpp|218|223|string_utils::format_string|snprintf(buffer, buf_size, fmt, args...);|the fmt parameter is passed directly to snprintf as the format string. if called with user-controlled format strings, attackers can leak stack data or cause crashes.|call to format_string with user-controlled first argument|validate that fmt contains only expected format specifiers, or use a whitelist approach for format strings.

buffer overflow in evaluate_expression with unchecked strcpy|buffer overflow|cwe-120|high|0,9|enhanced_calc.cpp|830|835|calculator::supercalc::evaluate_expression|if (expression.length() >= sizeof(input_buffer_)) { strcpy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str()); } else { string_utils::safe_string_copy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str(), sizeof(input_buffer_)); }|when expression length >= 1024 (stack_buffer_size), strcpy is used without bounds checking, overflowing the stack-allocated input_buffer_ and enabling potential code execution.|user input expression with length >= 1024 characters|always use safe_string_copy or strncpy, never fall back to strcpy. return error for expressions exceeding buffer size.->buffer overflow in evaluate_expression with unchecked strcpy|buffer overflow|cwe-120|high|0,9|enhanced_calc.cpp|830|835|calculator::supercalc::evaluate_expression|if (expression.length() >= sizeof(input_buffer_)) { strcpy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str()); } else { string_utils::safe_string_copy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str(), sizeof(input_buffer_)); }|when expression length >= 1024 (stack_buffer_size), strcpy is used without bounds checking, overflowing the stack-allocated input_buffer_ and enabling potential code execution.|user input expression with length >= 1024 characters|always use safe_string_copy or strncpy, never fall back to strcpy. return error for expressions exceeding buffer size.

command injection in execute_admin_command|command injection|cwe-78|critical|0,95|enhanced_calc.cpp|602|609|admin::adminconsole::execute_admin_command|if (command.substr(0, 4) == "exec") { std::string cmd = command.substr(5); std::cout << "executing: " << cmd << "\n"; system(cmd.c_str()); }|user-controlled command string is passed directly to system(). attacker can execute arbitrary os commands by crafting input like 'exec ; rm -rf /' or 'exec cat /etc/passwd'.|authenticated admin user sends command starting with 'exec ' followed by malicious shell commands|implement strict command whitelisting, use execve with argument arrays instead of system(), or remove the exec functionality entirely.->command injection in execute_admin_command|command injection|cwe-78|critical|0,95|enhanced_calc.cpp|602|609|admin::adminconsole::execute_admin_command|if (command.substr(0, 4) == "exec") { std::string cmd = command.substr(5); system(cmd.c_str()); }|user-controlled command string is passed directly to system(). attacker can execute arbitrary os commands by crafting input like 'exec ; rm -rf /' or 'exec cat /etc/passwd'.|authenticated admin user sends command starting with 'exec ' followed by malicious shell commands|implement strict command whitelisting, use execve with argument arrays instead of system(), or remove the exec functionality entirely.

command injection in append_override_audit_event|command injection|cwe-78|medium|0,75|enhanced_calc.cpp|622|633|admin::adminconsole::append_override_audit_event|const char* temp_dir = std::getenv("temp"); std::string audit_path = std::string(temp_dir ? temp_dir : ".") + "\\supercalc_emergency.log"; std::string command = "cmd /c echo admin override logged >> " + audit_path; system(command.c_str());|environment variables temp/tmpdir are concatenated into a system command without sanitization. in shared environments, attackers could set these to inject commands.|attacker-controlled temp or tmpdir environment variables when emergency override is triggered|use c++ file i/o (std::ofstream) instead of system() to write audit logs, avoiding shell interpretation.->command injection in append_override_audit_event|command injection|cwe-78|medium|0,75|enhanced_calc.cpp|622|633|admin::adminconsole::append_override_audit_event|const char* temp_dir = std::getenv("temp"); std::string audit_path = std::string(temp_dir ? temp_dir : ".") + "\\supercalc_emergency.log"; std::string command = "cmd /c echo admin override logged >> " + audit_path; system(command.c_str());|environment variables temp/tmpdir are concatenated into a system command without sanitization. in shared environments, attackers could set these to inject commands.|attacker-controlled temp or tmpdir environment variables when emergency override is triggered|use c++ file i/o (std::ofstream) instead of system() to write audit logs, avoiding shell interpretation.

truthAudit.Summary: Truth audit of Run 2 against 20 true vulnerabilities. Run 2 identified 15 findings covering 13 of the 20 true vulnerabilities. Found full coverage for 11 vulnerabilities, partial for 2 (SC-V3-013). Missed 7 vulnerabilities (SC-V3-012, SC-V3-014, SC-V3-015, SC-V3-016, SC-V3-017, SC-V3-018, SC-V3-019, SC-V3-020). Multiple severity underclaims and CWE misclassifications detected. Several findings in Run 2 do not map to truth items (format_string template, append_override_audit_event command injection).

Items.Notes: Run 2 finding #2 'Format string vulnerability in log_debug_message' directly quotes this evidence and describes the same vulnerability. Severity was rated High instead of Critical.
Run 2 finding #13 'Integer overflow in factorial function' quotes this evidence and describes overflow in fact computation. Severity was rated Medium instead of High.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: char* scratch = static_cast<char*>(memory::g_memory_pool.allocate(copy_len + 1)); memcpy(scratch, calc.data(), copy_len); thread_pool_->enqueue([scratch, copy_len] { ... for (size_t i = 0; i < copy_len; ++i) checksum += static_cast<unsigned char>(scratch[i]); ... });
Items.Notes: Run 2 finding #14 'Use-after-free in stage_deferred_history_flush' quotes this evidence and describes the UAF between g_memory_pool.cleanup() and async lambda. Severity was rated High instead of Critical.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: std::string validator_cmd = get_setting("validator_path", "/usr/bin/validator"); std::string config_file = get_setting("config_file", "default.conf"); std::string command = validator_cmd + " " + config_file; int result = system(command.c_str());
Items.Notes: Run 2 finding #7 'Command injection in validate_config via system()' quotes this evidence verbatim. Severity was rated High instead of Critical.
Run 2 finding #5 'Buffer overflow in evaluate_expression with unchecked strcpy' quotes this evidence and describes the stack buffer overflow in input_buffer_.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: long long old_value = value_; std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(mutex_); value_ = old_value + 1;
Items.Notes: Run 2 finding #12 'Race condition in ThreadSafeCounter increment' quotes this evidence verbatim and describes the race condition correctly. Severity matches (Medium).
Run 2 finding #10 'Authentication backdoor via EMERGENCY_OVERRIDE' quotes this evidence. Uses CWE-489 instead of CWE-511, but the vulnerability is correctly identified.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: std::string full_path = config_dir_ + filename; if (!std::filesystem::exists(full_path)) return false; std::ifstream file(full_path);
Items.Notes: Run 2 finding #15 'Path traversal in ConfigLoader::load_config' quotes this evidence and describes path traversal. Severity matches (Medium).

Run 2 finding #4 'Buffer overflow in ExpressionParser::parse via strcpy' quotes this evidence. Uses CWE-120 instead of CWE-122, and severity High instead of Critical, but vulnerability is correctly identified.
Run 2 finding #9 'Hardcoded administrator secret in source code' quotes this evidence verbatim. Severity was rated Medium instead of High.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: std::random_device rd; std::mt19937 gen(rd()); std::uniform_int_distribution<> dis(1000, 9999);
Items.Notes: Run 2 finding #11 'Weak session token generation' quotes this evidence. Uses CWE-330 instead of CWE-338, and severity Medium instead of High, but vulnerability is correctly identified.
Run 2 contains no finding about thread-unsafe static std::tm cached_time in log_debug_message (lines 230-231). This vulnerability was not identified.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: const char* temp_dir = std::getenv("TEMP"); std::string audit_path = std::string(temp_dir ? temp_dir : ".") + "\\supercalc_emergency.log"; std::string command = "cmd /C echo Admin override logged >> " + audit_path; system(command.c_str());
Items.Notes: Run 2 finding #8 'Command injection in append_override_audit_event' quotes this evidence but frames it as command injection via environment variables, not as insecure temporary file handling (CWE-377). The temp file aspect is not the primary focus.
Run 2 contains no finding about persistent authentication state (authenticated_, login_attempts_, session_token_ fields in AdminConsole). This vulnerability was not identified.
Run 2 contains no finding about unhandled exception in handle_variable_command where std::stod(tokens[3]) can throw. This vulnerability was not identified.
Run 2 contains no finding about integer underflow in MemoryPool::allocate where previous_size - size - SPLIT_METADATA_BYTES can underflow. This vulnerability was not identified.
Run 2 contains no finding about unbounded recursion in ExpressionParser::parse_expression -> parse_term -> parse_factor -> parse_expression with PAREN_OPEN. This vulnerability was not identified.

Run 2 contains no finding about data race in ExpressionEvaluator::evaluate_cached where result_cache_ is accessed without cache_mutex_ protection during thread_pool_->enqueue. This vulnerability was not identified.

Run 2 contains no finding about TOCTOU in ConfigLoader::load_config where std::filesystem::exists check (line 544) is followed by sleep_for (line 545) then std::ifstream open (line 546). This vulnerability was not identified.

Run 2 contains no finding about integer division by zero in calculate_route_score where 100 / static_cast<int>(token_length) can divide by zero when token_length is 0. This vulnerability was not identified.
